title: Crispy Sour Irish Pork Bake
description: A comforting Irish-inspired bake featuring seasoned pork chops wrapped in tangy cabbage and sauerkraut, finished with a crisp breadcrumb crust for a touch of crunch.
instructions: Pat the pork chops dry and season both sides with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
In a shallow dish, whisk the flour with a splash of water to create a thick paste.
Coat each pork chop in the flour paste, then dredge in panko breadcrumbs, pressing lightly to adhere.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in an oven‑proof skillet over medium‑high heat and sear the chops until golden brown, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Transfer the seared chops to a baking dish and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining olive oil, then sauté the sauerkraut, cabbage, apple cider vinegar, lemon juice, thyme, and a pinch of salt for 5 minutes, until the vegetables soften.
Spread the vegetable mixture evenly over the pork chops in the baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20–25 minutes, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C) and the breadcrumb topping is crisp.
Let the bake rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow juices to redistribute.
Serve warm, optionally garnished with fresh thyme or a squeeze of lemon.
